followup_notes.changes_from_prior_cycle: I keep the recommended verdict as promising but lower the overall score slightly from 7.0 to 6.8. The other reviews reinforced my concerns about missing quantitative values, MRI resolution diagnostics, and the BZ/BP citation error. I added figure-reference inconsistency, initial poloidal-field topology, gamma-law/cooling limitations, and the constant-angular-momentum profile as explicit caveats.
invalid_points_from_other_reviewers: I would not mark the main PPI trend as 'well supported' from the supplied text, because the key quantitative table and enhancement factors are not locally auditable.

The objection that the absence of a spin-flip needs a rigorous analytic derivation seems weak; it is a reported simulation outcome and only needs clearer quantitative spin/accretion accounting, not a separate theory, for this paper's scope.
I treat the widespread missing numbers primarily as a bundle/PDF-rendering audit blocker rather than proven manuscript defects until a human checks the PDF.
valid_points_from_other_reviewers: The BZ mechanism appears to be cited as Blandford and Payne (1982), and Blandford-Znajek (1977) appears absent; this is a real correction, not just stylistic.
The EM luminosity/ejecta figure references appear inconsistent with the supplied figure numbering and should be fixed or checked in the PDF.
The missing MRI quality factor and lack of convergence evidence are central technical gaps for a turbulence-driven interpretation.
The purely poloidal seed field materially caveats the claim that jet formation is robust.
The gamma-law adiabatic EOS and lack of cooling/microphysics limit astrophysical extrapolation to kilonovae, AGN flares, and detector relevance.
The nearly constant specific-angular-momentum profile is a meaningful confounder when attributing PPI dominance mainly to self-gravity.

main_claims.claim: These are the first self-consistent GRMHD simulations of tilted, self-gravitating black-hole disk systems with massive disks.
main_claims.notes: The literature framing is credible: prior cited tilted GRMHD work used fixed backgrounds/non-self-gravitating disks, while prior self-gravitating tilted work was hydrodynamic. I would not certify the priority claim without a human literature check, but the novelty claim is plausible.

main_claims.claim: The nonaxisymmetric PPI-like mode persists in all four configurations, with magnetic fields damping the aligned/small-tilt cases and enhancing the antialigned A4 case.
main_claims.notes: The qualitative trend is consistently described and appears supported by Fig. 4 and Table 1, but Table 1 and several quoted factors are not legible in the supplied text. The mode identification is also inherited largely from prior work.
main_claims.paper_location: Results around Fig. 4 and Table 1; Discussion

main_claims.claim: MRI turbulence develops faster than the PPI and causally accelerates angular-momentum transport and nonlinear instability growth in misaligned cases.
main_claims.notes: The timescale argument and accretion histories are plausible, but the paper does not show enough direct MRI-quality, stress, angular-momentum-flux, or convergence diagnostics to make the causal chain fully secure.
main_claims.paper_location: Results paragraphs after Table 1; Supplemental Accretion section

main_claims.claim: All models launch magnetically driven collimated outflows consistent with the Blandford-Znajek mechanism, with tilt-dependent morphology.
main_claims.notes: The morphology, magnetization, and horizon-threading field descriptions support jet formation. The BZ interpretation needs stronger diagnostics and the manuscript appears to cite Blandford-Payne rather than Blandford-Znajek for the relevant scaling. The purely poloidal seed field also limits claims of robustness.

main_claims.claim: The GW signals track the nonaxisymmetric dynamics and may be detectable for selected mass/distance scalings.
main_claims.notes: The qualitative amplitude trends follow the mode-amplitude narrative, but detectability claims require details on waveform extraction, duration/windowing, source orientation, SNR integration, scaling assumptions, and numerical values that are not available in the supplied text.

missing_assumptions.assumption: The fastest-growing MRI wavelength is resolved throughout the disk regions that drive the reported turbulence and accretion.
missing_assumptions.paper_location: Initial data and numerical methods; Results MRI/PPI timescale discussion
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: If MRI is under-resolved, the central claim that physical MRI turbulence modifies PPI growth is weakened.

missing_assumptions.assumption: The initial magnetic seeding is a weak, controlled perturbation of the hydrodynamic equilibrium.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: Uncontrolled initial transients could mimic early compression, accretion, and mode amplification.

missing_assumptions.assumption: The nearly constant specific-angular-momentum torus is representative enough for conclusions about tilt and self-gravity.
missing_assumptions.paper_location: Initial data; Results comparison to tilted disk tearing studies
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: Constant-j tori are especially PPI-prone, so the result may not extend to more Keplerian, cooled, or thinner disks.

missing_assumptions.assumption: The initially purely poloidal magnetic field does not predetermine the qualitative jet outcome.
missing_assumptions.paper_location: Initial data and numerical methods; Results on outflows
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: Large-scale poloidal flux strongly favors horizon-threading jets, so robustness across tilt angles is not the same as robustness across magnetic topologies.

missing_assumptions.assumption: The ideal-gas gamma-law evolution without cooling, radiation transport, or neutrino physics is adequate for the astrophysical scenarios invoked.
missing_assumptions.paper_location: Initial data and numerical methods; Results on ejecta and EM counterparts
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: Thermodynamics can alter disk thickness, MRI saturation, PPI behavior, ejecta mass, and EM-counterpart interpretation.

missing_assumptions.assumption: The finite simulation duration captures saturated or persistent behavior rather than early transient evolution.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: Claims about persistent modes, quasimonochromatic GWs, and jet robustness need sufficient post-transient evolution.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Are the missing numerical values and broken table entries present in the PDF/source, especially Table 1 growth rates, pattern speeds, enhancement factors, and model parameters?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: The supplied arXiv HTML bundle contains many LaTeXML-like omissions; a human must inspect the PDF or source.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Are the MRI quality factors, convergence behavior, Maxwell/Reynolds stresses, angular-momentum fluxes, and horizon magnetic flux diagnostics available outside the supplied text?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: These diagnostics are essential for the central mechanism but may exist in figures, supplemental files, or prior-code validation not captured here.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Does Table 1 quantitatively support the stated A4 growth-rate amplification and pattern-speed reduction with credible fit windows and uncertainties?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: The numerical entries and fitting details are not auditable from the supplied text.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Is the 'first self-consistent GRMHD treatment' claim accurate relative to recent tilted, self-gravitating, dynamical-spacetime GRMHD literature?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: This requires domain-specific literature knowledge beyond the visible citation list.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Do the jet diagnostics actually demonstrate Blandford-Znajek extraction rather than a disk wind or magnetic-tower outflow?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: The supplied text lacks the relevant horizon-energy-flux, field-line-rotation, and magnetic-flux diagnostics.

novelty_assessment: I keep the novelty assessment high but somewhat qualified. The combination of dynamical spacetime, disk self-gravity, GRMHD, and spin-disk tilt appears to fill a real gap relative to the cited literature, and the antialigned enhancement of the nonaxisymmetric mode is a nontrivial result. The work is also an incremental extension of the authors' prior hydrodynamic model family, with only four idealized configurations, so the novelty is strongest as a first demonstration rather than as a broad parameter-space result.
score_rationale: The paper remains a promising numerical-relativity/GRMHD contribution with real novelty and an interesting physical result, especially if the PDF contains the missing numerical values. I lower the score slightly after cycle 2 because the other reviews identify additional valid caveats: figure-reference problems, field-topology dependence of the jet claim, EOS/cooling limitations, and the constant-j torus as a confounder for PPI dominance. Technical soundness is limited mainly by missing MRI resolution/convergence/stress diagnostics and over-strong causal language, not by a demonstrated fatal flaw.
summary: The paper reports dynamical-spacetime GRMHD simulations of four magnetized, massive black-hole disk models with spin-disk misalignment, using the hydrodynamic self-gravitating models of Tsokaros et al. (2022) as baselines. I still view the work as promising and likely novel, especially for the simultaneous MRI/PPI evolution in tilted self-gravitating disks. After reading the other reviews, I keep my main caution: the manuscript's central physical story is plausible but not fully auditable from the supplied text because key numerical values and diagnostics are missing or rendered incorrectly, and because several causal/BZ/detectability claims outrun the displayed evidence. I add two useful caveats from the other reviewers: the constant-angular-momentum torus and purely poloidal initial field make the PPI and jet outcomes less general than the discussion sometimes implies.

technical_objections.evidence: The bundle has blanks or missing values for disk-to-BH mass ratios, spin, tilt angles, magnetic-to-gas pressure ratio, EOS parameter, domain/resolution, Table 1 growth rates and pattern speeds, enhancement factors, luminosities, ejecta fractions, distances, and detector scalings. This is probably an arXiv HTML conversion artifact, but it still prevents this review from verifying the quantitative claims.
technical_objections.objection: Key numerical and physical parameters are missing or illegible in the supplied paper text, blocking independent audit of central claims.
technical_objections.paper_location: Abstract; Initial data and numerical methods; Table 1; Results; Supplemental Fig. 11 caption

technical_objections.evidence: I do not see MRI quality factors, fastest-growing MRI wavelength versus grid spacing, Maxwell/Reynolds stress histories, alpha estimates, convergence tests, or angular momentum flux decomposition. Other reviewers correctly flagged the missing MRI Q-factor as a standard GRMHD validation diagnostic.
technical_objections.objection: The MRI-driven explanation is underdiagnosed for a paper whose main mechanism depends on resolved turbulence.
technical_objections.paper_location: Initial data and numerical methods; Results paragraphs beginning 'Magnetic turbulence triggers massive accretion...' and 'The hierarchy between MRI and PPI timescales...'

technical_objections.evidence: The text says the luminosity is consistent with BZ scaling but cites Blandford and Payne (1982), which is a disk-wind mechanism, while Blandford-Znajek (1977) is absent from the reference list. The paper mainly shows morphology, magnetization, and luminosity, not horizon energy extraction, field-line angular velocity, or horizon flux evolution.
technical_objections.objection: The identification of the jets as Blandford-Znajek powered is not fully demonstrated and the cited classic mechanism appears wrong.
technical_objections.paper_location: Results paragraph on polar funnel and EM luminosity; References entry 'Blandford and Payne (1982)'

technical_objections.evidence: The seed magnetic field is added to non-magnetized equilibrium tori, so the magnetized models are not exact equilibria. Early compression and accretion could partly reflect magnetic seeding transients. The text also does not make clear whether grid, atmosphere, extraction, and fitting procedures are matched closely enough to support quantitative growth-rate comparisons.
technical_objections.objection: The comparison with hydrodynamic baselines may not cleanly isolate magnetization unless initial-data transients and numerical comparability are quantified.
technical_objections.paper_location: Initial data and numerical methods; Results around Fig. 4 and Supplemental Accretion

technical_objections.evidence: The paper uses nearly constant-specific-angular-momentum massive tori, a setup already favorable to PPI. Other reviewers correctly noted that the angular-momentum profile, disk thickness, and thermodynamics may be as important as self-gravity in explaining why global PPI-like behavior dominates rather than tearing.
technical_objections.objection: The broad claim that PPI dominates over disk tearing because of self-gravity is not disentangled from the nearly constant specific-angular-momentum torus setup.
technical_objections.paper_location: Initial data; Results paragraph contrasting with Liska et al. (2021); Discussion

technical_objections.evidence: The study has four models, one torus family, one EOS, one initial magnetic topology/strength, no radiation/neutrino microphysics, and short-duration idealized evolutions. Purely poloidal fields favor rapid large-scale flux accumulation, and gamma-law adiabatic thermodynamics limits claims about kilonovae, AGN flares, and detectability across mass scales.
technical_objections.objection: The jet robustness and multimessenger extrapolations are stronger than the narrow simulation sample supports.
technical_objections.paper_location: Results on outflows, EM luminosity, ejecta, GW spectra; Discussion

technical_objections.evidence: The main text refers to Fig. 11 for EM Poynting luminosity and ejecta inset, while the supplied bundle labels the EM luminosity plot as Figure 6 and the supplemental GW characteristic-strain plot as Figure 11. This is likely a referencing or conversion issue rather than a scientific flaw.
technical_objections.objection: Figure references and figure numbering appear inconsistent, reducing local auditability.
technical_objections.paper_location: Results paragraph beginning 'The EM luminosity is in all cases'; Figure 6 caption; Supplemental Figure 11 caption

technical_objections.evidence: The text states that the models remain in the SANE regime, but I do not see a time series of the normalized horizon magnetic flux or a clear threshold comparison. This matters because the paper contrasts its dynamics with MAD tilted-disk simulations.
technical_objections.objection: The SANE rather than MAD characterization is asserted more than demonstrated in the supplied text.
technical_objections.paper_location: Results discussion comparing with Chatterjee et al. (2025)
